vocab.meaning: chấp nhận đối mặt với một tình huống khó khăn/không thoải mái
vocab.example: When I see a sea of brake lights, I just have to bite the bullet and accept that I'll be late for my appointment.

vocab.meaning: với tốc độ chậm như sên
vocab.example: If the traffic is moving at a snail's pace, I usually put on a podcast to keep my mind occupied.

vocab.meaning: giữ tinh thần lạc quan, không nản chí
vocab.example: It's easy to get frustrated in gridlock, but I try to keep my chin up by remembering that everyone is in the same boat.

vocab.word: kill two birds with one stone
vocab.meaning: một mũi tên trúng hai đích
vocab.example: I try to kill two birds with one stone by listening to educational audiobooks while waiting for the traffic to clear.
I’ve learned that there’s no point in losing my cool over traffic, as it only makes the experience more stressful.

vocab.meaning: quan sát thế giới xung quanh trôi qua (một cách tĩnh lặng)
vocab.example: As a passenger, I find it quite relaxing to just sit back and watch the world go by when we are stuck in a jam.

vocab.meaning: đón nhận một khó khăn một cách bình thản và dễ dàng
vocab.example: Traffic congestion is inevitable in this city, so I've learned to take it in my stride and not let it ruin my day.
I usually just turn up the air conditioning and chill out with some lo-fi music until the bottleneck eases up.
Singing along to high-energy songs at the top of my lungs helps me blow off steam when I'm feeling restless in the car.

vocab.meaning: để mọi thứ diễn ra theo tự nhiên (không can thiệp)
vocab.example: Sometimes there are no shortcuts, so I just sit tight and let nature take its course until the road clears.
